title: 1.2: Reports the shell process PID (e.g., numeric format like 5597).
1.3: Reports kitty holds PTY master device (e.g., /dev/pts/ptmx, /dev/ptmx on a file descriptor).
1.4: Reports shell uses PTY slave device (e.g., /dev/pts/0, similar /dev/pts/N) on stdin/stdout/stderr (fds 0, 1, 2).
1.5: Reports echo test123 input involves small byte transfer (e.g., ~20-30 bytes).
1.6: Explains kitty uses event-driven I/O mechanism (e.g., poll, similar function) to monitor PTY master file descriptor.
1.7: Reports yes hello produces significantly larger I/O volume than echo test123 (e.g., tens of megabytes vs tens of bytes).
1.8: Reports yes hello involves high read syscall frequency (e.g., hundreds of thousands to ~1 million read syscalls within seconds).
1.9: Identifies the function responsible for reading data from PTY master file descriptor in child-monitor.c (e.g., read_bytes, equivalent function).
1.10: Identifies VT parsing functions in vt-parser.c that handle input processing (e.g., consume_input, consume_normal, consume_esc, consume_csi, similar state handlers).
1.11: Reports the exact command line of the spawned shell process (e.g., /bin/bash --posix, /bin/zsh, similar shell invocation).
1.12: Reports the buffer size used for PTY reads (e.g., 1 MB, 1048576 bytes, similar large buffer).
1.13: Reports the typical byte count per read during high-volume output (e.g., ~80-100 bytes per read, small chunks despite large buffer).
1.14: Reports the file descriptor number kitty uses to read from the PTY master (e.g., fd 10, similar single-digit fd).
